title: Material Behavior Model (MBM) — Ontology
renamedFrom: Material World Model / Material State Space
principle: Everything in chemistry is a state transition. The model does not describe a 'material' — it describes BEHAVIOUR: Matter + Energy + Environment + Time. A material is defined by (1) which STATE it is in, (2) what DRIVER(S) move it, and (3) the RESULT. Materials exist inside a State Space; documents/patents/TGA/DSC/FTIR/experiments are EVIDENCE that updates the model — not the model.
note: v1.1 evolved under the Ontology Validation Suite (a deliberate falsification attempt across 5 driver families). v1.0 did NOT survive unchanged — see MBM-OntologyValidationSuite.md for what broke and why. Documentation only.

entities.definition: A distinct condition a material can be in. The unit of the model.
A directed change from one State to another under one or MORE Drivers, via a Mechanism.
What moves a material between states. Each belongs to exactly one category.
entities.note: v1.1 finding: the proposed 4-category taxonomy (Energy/Mass/Chemical/Mechanical) had no home for `time`; a 5th category `temporal` was added. `time` is an exposure/kinetic driver, not a force/flux.

entities.definition: The epistemic status of a transition — how much we know it happens. One of seven.
entities.note: Replaces v1.0's binary isHypothesis. 'impossible' = no known mechanism (e.g. glass + uv -> melting). 'unknown' = State A -> ??? (a gap, not an answer).

entities.definition: A universal law every transition must respect regardless of material, unless it declares an Exception with justification.
entities.note: The model does not merely describe transitions — it CONSTRAINS them to be physically consistent. A violation without an Exception is flagged 'inconsistent'.

entities.definition: Generated automatically from an 'unknown' transition. Carries cost / impact / priority so experiment planning is gap-driven.

Per-transition uncertainty 0..1 (0 = observed+replicated+mechanism-supported; 0.5 = hypothesis; 1 = unknown). Drives 'uncertainty clouds' in simulation and 'run an experiment instead of answering' when high.
The physical/chemical mechanism realising a transition.
A boundary present in a state or created by a transition.
A measurable characteristic that changes across transitions.
A source observation (TGA/DSC/FTIR/XRD/SEM/patent/experiment) that UPDATES a State or Transition. Not the model — the update to it.
A physical/chemical/process/economic/regulatory boundary a state or transition must respect.
A Transition validated into a general rule via human-gated validation (FSCTM).

governance: Provenance required for observed/replicated/mechanism_supported — no invented chemistry (Principle 7).
predicted/hypothesized/unknown transitions are NOT facts; unknown generates a KnowledgeGap, never an answer.
Every transition must respect the 5 Invariants unless it declares an Exception with justification.
A Transition becomes a Law only via human-gated validation (FSCTM).
